growth_records.cite: Kulyashov et al. Modification and analysis of context-specific genome-scale metabolic models: methane-utilizing microbial chassis as a case study. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01105-24. [PMC11748545]
growth_records.snippet: cell samples... were collected from steady-state bioreactor cultures growing in nitrate mineral media at pH 8.7-9 with or without La supplementation. ...versus 1.12 +/- 0.09 obtained for chemostat cultures. [Table 1: Ca growth rate 0.05 h-1
growth_records.notes: Reported experimental mu=0.05 h-1 (Ca condition, steady-state bioreactor). td=ln2/mu=13.86 h. O2:CH4 consumption ratio 1.12+/-0.09 recorded as a stoichiometric relationship (from text comparing model 1.22/1.273 vs experimental 1.12). pH midpoint 8.85 of stated 8.7-9. Absolute CH4/O2 fluxes NOT recorded: the values -11.7, -2.85 (Ca), -6.84 (La) mmol/gDCW/h in Table 4 are FBA context-specific model constraint bounds, not experimental measurements. medium_composition_stated=false (Pi3% NMS full recipe in ref 25).

growth_records.cite: Kulyashov et al. Modification and analysis of context-specific genome-scale metabolic models: methane-utilizing microbial chassis as a case study. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01105-24. [PMC11748545]
growth_records.snippet: [Table 1: La-BR1/LaBR2 growth rate 0.07 h-1; CH4/O2 consumption 1.28 +/- 0.01] ... a more significant reduction of the biomass yield was observed during growth in La-supplemented media, confirming a higher flux through formate.
growth_records.notes: Reported experimental mu=0.07 h-1 (La condition, steady-state bioreactor). td=ln2/0.07=9.90 h. O2:CH4 ratio 1.28+/-0.01 (Table 1). Absolute CH4 uptake -6.84 mmol/gDCW/h in Table 4 is an FBA model constraint, not an experimental measurement, so not recorded as uptake. pH midpoint 8.85 of 8.7-9. Temp 30 C = stated routine incubation temp.
